title: Vegan vs Vegetarian
shortAnswer: Vegetarians avoid meat but eat dairy and eggs, while vegans avoid all animal products. Both offer health benefits but vegans need more careful nutrient planning.

keyDifferences.label: B-12 Supplementation
keyDifferences.entityAValue: Can get B-12 from dairy and eggs

prosA: Easier access to B-12, calcium, and complete proteins through dairy and eggs
Less supplementation required for essential nutrients
Still provides significant health benefits when properly planned
Professional organizations endorse well-planned vegetarian diets
consA: Still requires attention to iron, zinc, and omega-3 fatty acids
Children need careful monitoring of calories, protein, and key vitamins
prosB: Eliminates all animal products for ethical consistency
Offers significant health benefits for disease prevention and management
Supported by current research for plant-based nutrition
Professional organizations endorse well-planned vegan diets
consB: Needs more attention to supplementation and food fortification
Requires careful planning for complete protein sources
verdict: Both diets offer significant health benefits when properly planned, with professional endorsement for disease prevention. Vegetarian diets provide easier access to certain nutrients through dairy and eggs, while vegan diets require more careful supplementation but offer complete elimination of animal products.
